channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: From OpenAI to Nvidia, here’s a list of recent multibillion-dollar AI deals
item.description: Nvidia has agreed to license technology from AI startup Groq for use in some of its artificial intelligence chips, marking the chipmaker’s largest deal and underscoring its push to strengthen competitiveness amid surging demand.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: After LLMs and agents, the next AI frontier: video language models
item.description: Tesla’s viral videos show its Optimus humanoid robot serving drinks to guests — a glimpse of AI in the real world that a new AI innovation, called world models, is expected to make more reliable. (For one, humanoid robots will do a better job in navigating and serving people their custom drinks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Guess where all the NAND flash components are going? The 8th (yes, eighth) 245TB SSD has been announced amidst race to quench AI storage thirst of hyperscalers
item.description: DapuStor announces a 245TB class SSD as AI workloads continue to shape flash storage priorities in hyperscale data centers.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: AI-created ransomware and NFC attacks lead the surge in new cyberattacks - here's how you can stay safe this holidays
item.description: AI is no longer being used just to craft convincing phishing emails - it's being used to build ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Cameroon vs Gabon Free Streams: How to watch AFCON 2025 Group F opener, TV channels, preview
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch Cameroon vs Gabon in the 2025 AFCON from anywhere in the world.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Shapland, TechRadar

item.title: Waymo to update software across fleet after major power failure
item.description: Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo said it would strengthen its automated software to better handle power outages after its self-driving taxis froze and caused traffic jams during a major electricity failure last weekend in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel shares fall on report Nvidia halts production test
item.description: Intel Corp. shares fell Wednesday after a report said that Nvidia Corp. halted a test to use Intel's production process to make advanced chips.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: These malicious Google Chrome extensions have stolen data from over 170 sites - find out if you're affected
item.description: Two Chrome extensions were found eavesdropping on people's browsing, stealing login credentials and payment card information.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft is not rewriting Windows in Rust
item.description: A job posting by a Microsoft engineer sparked excitement about a project “to eliminate every line of C and C++ from Microsoft by 2030”, replacing it with Rust — but alas for fans of the memory-safe programming language, it turns out this is a personal goal, not a corporate one, and Rust isn’t necess
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FCC bans new Chinese-made drones, citing security risks
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission says it will ban new foreign-made drones, a move that will keep new Chinese-made drones out of the U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US cyber progress isn’t stalled
item.description: Beneath the policy layer, the technical and strategic modernization of U.S. cybersecurity is actually accelerating faster than ever. The post US cyber progress isn’t stalled — it’s evolving first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Sean Davis, Federal News Network

item.title: Lessons from the frontlines of government finance
item.description: Teresa Hunter, a former CFO at the IRS, explains three things every federal agency leader should embrace to drive process and technology improvements. The post Lessons from the frontlines of government finance first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Google sues data scraping company
item.description: Google is now suing US data scraping company Serpapi for using hundreds of millions of fake search queries to bypass Google’s protection system and illegally obtain copyrighted material from search results, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 old programming languages developers won’t quit
item.description: The computer revolution has always been driven by the new and the next. The hype-mongers have trained us to assume that the latest iteration of ideas will be the next great leap forward. Some, though, are quietly stepping off the hype train.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 6 AI breakthroughs that will define 2026
item.description: The most significant advances in artificial intelligence next year won’t come from building larger models but from making AI systems smarter, more collaborative, and more reliable.
item.dc:creator: Info World
